docs.title: Hysteria
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "hysteria"
  type: hysteria
  server: server.com
  port: 443
  # ports: 1000,2000-3000,4000 # port cannot be omitted
  auth-str: yourpassword
  # obfs: obfs_str
  # alpn:
  #   - h3
  protocol: udp # Supports udp/wechat-video/faketcp
  up: "30 Mbps" # Defaults to Mbps when the unit is omitted
  down: "200 Mbps" # Defaults to Mbps when the unit is omitted
  # sni: server.com
  # skip-cert-verify: false
  # name-cert-verify: example.com
  # recv-window-conn: 12582912
  # recv-window: 52428800
  # disable_mtu_discovery: false
  # fingerprint: xxxx # Configuring a fingerprint enables SSL pinning. You can get it with openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256 -inform pem -in yourcert.pem
  # fast-open: true # Enables Fast Open to reduce connection setup latency. Default: false

Common fields

TLS fields

docs.title: Hysteria2
docs.text:
Configuration Reference

proxies:
- name: "hysteria2"
  type: hysteria2
  server: server.com
  port: 443
  ports: 443-8443
  hop-interval: 30
  password: yourpassword
  up: "30 Mbps"
  down: "200 Mbps"
  # bbr-profile: "" # Available: "standard", "conservative", "aggressive". Default: "standard"
  obfs: salamander # Defaults to empty. If filled, obfs will be enabled. Currently supports `salamander` and `gecko`.
  obfs-password: yourpassword
  # obfs-min-packet-size: 512
  # obfs-max-packet-size: 1200
  sni: server.com
  skip-cert-verify: false
  name-cert-verify: example.com
  fingerprint: xxxx # Configuring the fingerprint provides SSL pinning. Obtain it with: openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256 -inform pem -in yourcert.pem
  alpn:
    - h3
  # realm-opts:
  #   enable: true # Must be turned on manually
  #   server-url: https://realm.hy2.io
  #   token: public
  #   realm-id: my-cabin-1f3a8c2e9b
  #   stun-servers:
  #     - stun.nextcloud.com:3478
  #     - stun.sip.us:3478
  #     - global.stun.twilio.com:3478
  #   # The following supports entering TLS configuration for the server-url (sni, skip-cert-verify, name-cert-verify, fingerprint, certificate, private-key, alpn)
  #   # skip-cert-verify： false
  #   # name-cert-verify: example.com
  #   # ......
  ###Special quic-go options. Do not modify them unless you know what you are doing.###
  # initial-stream-receive-window： 8388608
  # max-stream-receive-window： 8388608
  # initial-connection-receive-window： 20971520
  # max-connection-receive-window： 20971520

Common Fields

TLS Fields

docs.title: ports
docs.text: Configuring this enables port jumping, ignoring port. Refer to Port Range for format.

docs.title: hop-interval
docs.text:
Port hop interval, in seconds, default is 30.

Entering "15-30" will randomly select one value as the switching interval each time. Only one range is supported (commas are not allowed).

docs.title: password
docs.text: Authentication password.

docs.title: up/down
docs.text: Brutal rate control; if no unit is specified, the default is Mbps.

docs.title: obfs
docs.text: QUIC traffic obfuscator type. Can be set to salamander or gecko. If left blank, obfuscation is disabled.

docs.title: obfs-password
docs.text: QUIC traffic obfuscator password.

docs.title: obfs-min-packet-size
docs.text: Minimum wire packet size (in bytes). Restricted to gecko only.

docs.title: obfs-max-packet-size
docs.text: Maximum wire packet size (in bytes). Restricted to gecko only.

docs.title: MASQUE
docs.text:
proxies:
# masque
- name: "masque"
  type: masque
  server: server.com
  port: 443
  private-key: BASE64_ENCODED_PRIVATE_KEY
  public-key: BASE64_ENCODED_PUBLIC_KEY
  ip: 172.16.0.2/32
  ipv6: fd00::2/128
  mtu: 1280
  udp: true
  # sni: example.com
  # Identifier of an outbound proxy. When non-empty, connections are sent through the specified proxy.
  # dialer-proxy: "ss1"
  # remote-dns-resolve: true # Force remote DNS resolution, default is false
  # dns: [ 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8 ] # Effective only when remote-dns-resolve is true
  # congestion-controller: bbr # Disabled by default
  # bbr-profile: "" # Available: "standard", "conservative", "aggressive". Default: "standard"
  # handshake-timeout: 30

# masque-h3-l4proxy
- name: "masque-h3-l4proxy"
  type: masque
  server: server.com
  port: 443
  private-key: BASE64_ENCODED_PRIVATE_KEY
  public-key: BASE64_ENCODED_PUBLIC_KEY
  udp: false # l4proxy mode currently does not support UDP
  network: h3-l4proxy
  # remote-dns-resolve: true # Force remote DNS resolution, default is false
  # dns: [ tls://1.1.1.1, tls://1.0.0.1 ] # Effective only when remote-dns-resolve is true
  # congestion-controller: bbr # Disabled by default
  # handshake-timeout: 30

# masque-h2
- name: "masque-h2"
  type: masque
  server: server.com
  port: 443
  private-key: BASE64_ENCODED_PRIVATE_KEY
  public-key: BASE64_ENCODED_PUBLIC_KEY
  ip: 172.16.0.2/32
  ipv6: fd00::2/128
  mtu: 1280
  udp: true
  # sni: example.com
  network: h2
  # Identifier of an outbound proxy. When non-empty, connections are sent through the specified proxy.
  # dialer-proxy: "ss1"
  # remote-dns-resolve: true # Force remote DNS resolution, default is false
  # dns: [ 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8 ] # Effective only when remote-dns-resolve is true
  # handshake-timeout: 30

docs.title: Get MASQUE configuration
docs.text:
Generate MASQUE configuration using the usque tool.

Common Fields

docs.title: private-key
docs.text: Required, base64-encoded ECDSA private key.

docs.title: public-key
docs.text:
Required, base64-encoded ECDSA public key (server-side public key).

Note

Remove PEM header/footer markers such as -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----, -----END PUBLIC KEY-----, and PEM line breaks \n.

docs.title: ip
docs.text: Local IPv4 address in CIDR format (e.g., 172.16.0.2/32).

docs.title: ipv6
docs.text: Local IPv6 address in CIDR format (e.g., fd00::2/128).

docs.title: mtu
docs.text: MTU size for the TUN device, defaults to 1280.

docs.title: udp
docs.text: Whether to enable UDP support, defaults to false.

docs.title: remote-dns-resolve
docs.text: Whether to enable remote DNS resolution through the MASQUE tunnel.

docs.title: dns
docs.text: List of remote DNS servers, takes effect when remote-dns-resolve is enabled.

docs.title: congestion-controller
docs.text: Overrides the default congestion control algorithm. Disabled by default. Available values include bbr.

docs.title: network
docs.text:
Optional. Defaults to quic. For masque-h2, set it to h2. For h3-l4proxy mode, set it to h3-l4proxy.

Note

h3-l4proxy mode currently does not support UDP.

docs.title: handshake-timeout
docs.text: Handshake timeout in seconds. The default value is 0, meaning only the outer connection timeout is used.

docs.title: Mieru
docs.text:
proxies:
  - name: mieru
    type: mieru
    server: server
    port: 2999
    port-range: 2090-2099
    transport: TCP
    username: user
    password: password
    multiplexing: MULTIPLEXING_LOW
    handshake-mode: HANDSHAKE_STANDARD
    traffic-pattern: ""

Common fields

docs.title: port-range
docs.text: Port range. Cannot be written together with port.

docs.title: transport
docs.text: Protocol. Currently supports TCP and UDP.

docs.title: multiplexing
docs.text: Multiplexing mode. Available values include MULTIPLEXING_OFF, MULTIPLEXING_LOW, MULTIPLEXING_MIDDLE, and MULTIPLEXING_HIGH. MULTIPLEXING_OFF disables multiplexing. Default: MULTIPLEXING_LOW.

docs.title: handshake-mode
docs.text: Handshake mode. HANDSHAKE_STANDARD (default) waits for the handshake to complete; HANDSHAKE_NO_WAIT enables a 0-RTT handshake.

docs.title: traffic-pattern
docs.text: A base64 string used to fine-tune network behavior. See the official mieru documentation for the format.

docs.title: OpenVPN
docs.text:
proxies:
  - name: "openvpn"
    type: openvpn
    server: vpn.example.com
    port: 1194
    proto: udp
    # auth-user-pass mode
    username: "user"
    password: "pass"
    # Certificate mode (Alternative to the above)
    # cert: |
    #   -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
    #   ...
    #   -----END CERTIFICATE-----
    # key: |
    #   -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----
    #   ...
    #   -----END PRIVATE KEY-----
    # tls-auth: |
    #   -----BEGIN OpenVPN Static key V1-----
    #   ...
    #   -----END OpenVPN Static key V1-----
    # key-direction: "1"
    ca: |
      -----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
      MIIB...example
      -----END CERTIFICATE-----
    # tls-crypt: |
    #  -----BEGIN OpenVPN Static key V1-----
    #  ...
    #  -----END OpenVPN Static key V1-----
    # tls-crypt-v2: |
    #  -----BEGIN OpenVPN tls-crypt-v2 client key-----
    #  ...
    #  -----END OpenVPN tls-crypt-v2 client key-----
    # peer-info:
    #   IV_HWADDR: "52:54:00:ff:72:87"
    #   UV_DEVICE_ID: "laptop-001"
    # ping: 10
    # ping-restart: 60
    # handshake-timeout: 30
    # dev: tun
    # cipher: AES-128-GCM
    # data-ciphers: [AES-256-GCM, AES-128-GCM]
    # data-ciphers-fallback: AES-128-CBC
    # auth: SHA256
    # comp-lzo: "no"
    udp: true
    # mtu: 1500
    # dialer-proxy: "ss1"
    # remote-dns-resolve: true
    # dns: [ 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8 ]

Common Fields

docs.title: server
docs.text: Required, the address of the OpenVPN server.

docs.title: port
docs.text: Required, the port of the OpenVPN server. Defaults to 1194.

docs.title: proto
docs.text: Optional, protocol type. Supports udp or tcp. Defaults to udp.

docs.title: username / password
docs.text:
Optional, the username and password required for auth-user-pass authentication mode.

Note: Must choose between these or cert / key. Both pairs cannot be empty at the same time.

docs.title: ca
docs.text: Required, CA certificate content. Copy this from the <ca> tag in your .ovpn file.

docs.title: cert
docs.text: Optional, client certificate content. Copy this from the <cert> tag in your .ovpn file. Can be omitted when using username/password authentication.

docs.title: key
docs.text: Optional, client private key content. Copy this from the <key> tag in your .ovpn file. Can be omitted when using username/password authentication.

docs.title: tls-auth
docs.text: Optional, copy from the <tls-auth> tag in the .ovpn file. Mutually exclusive with tls-crypt / tls-crypt-v2.

docs.title: key-direction
docs.text: Optional, required when using tls-auth, supports "1" or "0". If left blank or as an empty string, it defaults to bidirectional mode.

docs.title: tls-crypt
docs.text: Optional, TLS encryption key. Copy this from the <tls-crypt> tag in your .ovpn file; do not include the tags themselves. Mutually exclusive with tls-auth / tls-crypt-v2.

docs.title: tls-crypt-v2
docs.text: Optional, copy the client key from the <tls-crypt-v2> tag in your .ovpn file; do not include the tags themselves. Mutually exclusive with tls-auth / tls-crypt.

docs.title: ping
docs.text: Optional, defaults to 0.

docs.title: ping-restart
docs.text: Optional, defaults to 0.

docs.title: peer-info
docs.text: Optional key-value pairs passed to the server as peer-info. They are appended after the built-in IV_VER/IV_PROTO/IV_CIPHERS values and can be used by the server for admission decisions.

docs.title: handshake-timeout
docs.text: Optional handshake timeout in seconds. The default value is 0, meaning only the outer connection timeout is used.

docs.title: dev
docs.text: Optional, virtual network interface type. Currently only tun is supported. Defaults to tun.

docs.title: cipher
docs.text: Optional, encryption method. Supports AES-128-GCM / AES-256-GCM / AES-128-CBC / AES-256-CBC / CHACHA20-POLY1305. Defaults to AES-128-GCM. AES-CBC will be treated as AES-128-CBC.

docs.title: data-ciphers
docs.text: Optional, data channel cipher negotiation list, sends IV_CIPHERS to the server; the cipher list pushed by the server is intersected with the local list, and the first matching item is selected.

docs.title: data-ciphers-fallback
docs.text: Optional, fallback cipher when negotiation fails (corresponds to --data-ciphers-fallback)

docs.title: auth
docs.text: Optional, data authentication algorithm. Supports MD5 /SHA1 / SHA256/ SHA384 / SHA512. Defaults to SHA256. AEAD ciphers will ignore the auth configuration.

docs.title: comp-lzo
docs.text: Optional, data compression method. Supported values: yes, no, adaptive.

docs.title: udp
docs.text: Optional, whether to use the UDP protocol. true for UDP, false for TCP.

docs.title: mtu
docs.text: Optional, Maximum Transmission Unit. Defaults to 1500.

docs.title: dialer-proxy
docs.text: Optional, specifies the identifier for an outbound proxy. If set, the OpenVPN connection will be established through the specified proxy.

docs.title: remote-dns-resolve
docs.text: Optional, whether to force remote DNS resolution. Defaults to false.

docs.title: dns
docs.text: Optional, only effective when remote-dns-resolve is set to true. Specifies a list of DNS server addresses.

docs.title: Rematch
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "rematch"
  type: rematch
  target-rematch-name: "rematch1"
  target-sub-rule: "sub-rule1"

Common fields

docs.title: target-rematch-name
docs.text: Overrides the original rematch-name in metadata. It can be matched with the REMATCH-NAME rule.

docs.title: target-sub-rule
docs.text: If set, matching continues with the specified sub-rule. If the name does not exist or is empty, matching falls back to the main rules.

docs.title: Mark and Continue Matching Main Rules
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "mark-streaming"
  type: rematch
  target-rematch-name: "streaming"

proxy-groups:
- name: "Streaming"
  type: select
  proxies:
    - ss1
    - ss2

rules:
  - REMATCH-NAME,streaming,Streaming
  - DOMAIN-SUFFIX,netflix.com,mark-streaming
  - MATCH,DIRECT

When a request first matches DOMAIN-SUFFIX,netflix.com,mark-streaming, mark-streaming writes rematch-name: streaming and enters rule matching again. On the next pass, REMATCH-NAME,streaming,Streaming matches first.

Note

Put the REMATCH-NAME rule before the rule that triggers rematch, otherwise the next pass can hit the same rematch outbound again and form a cycle.

docs.title: Jump to a Sub-Rule
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "use-ai-rules"
  type: rematch
  target-sub-rule: "ai-rules"

proxy-groups:
- name: "AI"
  type: select
  proxies:
    - ss1
    - ss2

rules:
  - DOMAIN-SUFFIX,openai.com,use-ai-rules
  - MATCH,DIRECT

sub-rules:
  ai-rules:
    - DOMAIN-SUFFIX,openai.com,AI
    - MATCH,DIRECT

After a request matches use-ai-rules, matching continues with the ai-rules sub-rule. When the sub-rule exists, it is recommended to include a MATCH fallback.

docs.title: ShadowQUIC
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: shadowquic
  server: www.example.com
  port: 10443
  type: shadowquic
  username: username
  password: password
  # sni: example.com
  # alpn: [h3]
  # quic-versions: [v1] # support v1/v2, default v1
  # udp-over-stream: false # UDP over stream, default false
  # zero-rtt: false
  # keep-alive-interval: 10000
  # congestion-controller: cubic
  # up: 100 Mbps
  # down: 100 Mbps
  # cwnd: 32
  # bbr-profile: "standard"
  # max-datagram-frame-size: 1400
  # max-open-streams: 1024
  # recv-window-conn: 0
  # recv-window: 0
  # disable-mtu-discovery: false

General Fields

TLS Fields

Tip

ShadowQUIC always enables JLS; jls-opts does not need to be configured separately.

docs.title: username
docs.text: Required, username used for ShadowQUIC authentication.

docs.title: password
docs.text: Required, password used for ShadowQUIC authentication.

docs.title: sni
docs.text: Optional, SNI used by the ShadowQUIC TLS handshake.

docs.title: alpn
docs.text: Optional, application-layer protocol negotiation list. Defaults to h3.

docs.title: quic-versions
docs.text: Optional, sets the supported QUIC versions. Available values: v1/v2. Default is v1.

docs.title: udp-over-stream
docs.text: Optional, sets whether to transmit UDP over stream. Default is false.

docs.title: zero-rtt
docs.text:
Optional, sets whether to enable QUIC 0-RTT (Zero Round Trip Time) handshake.

Warning

The 0-RTT path of the official server may read the user state before JLS authentication is completed (confirmed to affect v0.3.11); if you encounter connection failures or disconnections when using the official server, please disable zero-rtt on the server side.

docs.title: keep-alive-interval
docs.text: Optional, the interval time for sending keep-alive heartbeat packets to maintain connection activity, in milliseconds.

docs.title: congestion-controller
docs.text: Optional, sets the congestion control algorithm. Available values: cubic/new_reno/bbr. Default is cubic.

docs.title: up
docs.text: Optional, sets the client upload speed and enables Brutal negotiation through a mihomo private extension. Falls back to congestion-controller when unsupported.

docs.title: down
docs.text: Optional, sets the requested client download speed, capped by the Listener's up, and enables Brutal negotiation through a mihomo private extension. Falls back to congestion-controller when unsupported.

docs.title: cwnd
docs.text: Optional, sets the initial congestion window size. Default is 32.

docs.title: bbr-profile
docs.text: Optional, sets the aggressiveness profile for the BBR algorithm. Available values: standard/conservative/aggressive. Only effective when congestion-controller is set to bbr.

docs.title: max-datagram-frame-size
docs.text: Optional, sets the maximum allowed datagram frame size, in bytes. Default is 1400.

docs.title: max-open-streams
docs.text: Optional, sets the maximum number of concurrent streams that can be opened simultaneously. Default is 1024.

docs.title: recv-window-conn
docs.text: Optional, sets the receive window size for an individual stream. If set to 0, the internal default value will be used.

docs.title: recv-window
docs.text: Optional, sets the connection-level receive window size. If set to 0, the internal default value will be used.

docs.title: disable-mtu-discovery
docs.text: Optional, sets whether to disable Path MTU Discovery. If set to true, a fixed MTU size will be used.

docs.title: Snell
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "snell"
  type: snell
  server: server
  port: 44046
  psk: yourpsk
  # version: 4
  # udp: true
  # reuse: false
  # obfs-opts:
  #   mode: http
  #   host: bing.com

- name: "snell-shadow-tls"
  type: snell
  server: server
  port: 44046
  psk: yourpsk
  # version: 4
  # udp: true
  # reuse: false
  # client-fingerprint: chrome
  obfs-opts:
     mode: shadow-tls
     host: bing.com
     password: "shadow_tls_password"
     version: 2
     alpn: ["h2"]

- name: "snell-restls"
  type: snell
  server: server
  port: 44046
  psk: yourpsk
  # version: 4
  # client-fingerprint: chrome
  obfs-opts:
    mode: restls
    host: bing.com
    password: "restls_password"
    version-hint: tls13

- name: "snell-jls"
  type: snell
  server: server
  port: 44046
  psk: yourpsk
  # version: 4
  # client-fingerprint: chrome
  obfs-opts:
    mode: jls
    host: bing.com
    username: jls-user
    password: "jls_password"

Common Fields

docs.title: psk
docs.text: Required. Snell pre-shared key.

docs.title: version
docs.text: Snell version. Supports v1/2/3/4/5. UDP is only supported on v3/4/5.

docs.title: reuse
docs.text: Optional. Supports v4/5. Default is false.

docs.title: client-fingerprint
docs.text: Optional. Default is chrome.

docs.title: obfs-opts
docs.text: Snell obfuscation settings.

docs.title: obfs-opts.mode
docs.text: Snell obfuscation mode. Supports http/tls/shadow-tls/restls/jls.

docs.title: obfs-opts.host
docs.text: Snell obfuscation hostname.

docs.title: obfs-opts.password
docs.text: The ShadowTLS password. Required only when shadow-tls is used.

docs.title: obfs-opts.version
docs.text: The ShadowTLS version. Supported values are v1/2/3. Required only when shadow-tls is used.

docs.title: obfs-opts.alpn
docs.text: Supported values are h2 and http/1.1. Required only when shadow-tls is used.

docs.title: obfs-opts.username
docs.text: JLS username. Required only when jls is used.

docs.title: obfs-opts.version-hint
docs.text: Restls TLS version hint. Available values: tls12 / tls13.

docs.title: obfs-opts.restls-script
docs.text: Optional Restls carrier traffic script after the handshake.

docs.title: SOCKS
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "socks"
  type: socks5
  server: server
  port: 443
  # username: username
  # password: password
  # tls: true
  # fingerprint: xxxx
  # skip-cert-verify: true
  # name-cert-verify: example.com
  # udp: true
  # ip-version: ipv6

Common fields

TLS fields

docs.title: Shadowsocks
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "ss1"
  type: ss
  server: server
  port: 443
  cipher: aes-128-gcm
  password: "password"
  udp: true
  udp-over-tcp: false
  udp-over-tcp-version: 2
  ip-version: ipv4
  plugin: obfs
  plugin-opts:
    mode: tls
  smux:
    enabled: false

Common fields

docs.title: Cipher
docs.text: AESCHACHA2022 Blake3LEAOther Method aes-128-ctr aes-192-ctr aes-256-ctr aes-128-cfb aes-192-cfb aes-256-cfb aes-128-gcm aes-192-gcm aes-256-gcm aes-128-ccm aes-192-ccm aes-256-ccm aes-128-gcm-siv aes-256-gcm-siv Method chacha20-ietf chacha20 xchacha20 chacha20-ietf-poly1305 xchacha20-ietf-poly1305 chacha8-ietf-poly1305 xchacha8-ietf-poly1305 Method 2022-blake3-aes-128-gcm 2022-blake3-aes-256-gcm 2022-blake3-chacha20-poly1305 Method lea-128-gcm lea-192-gcm lea-256-gcm Method rabbit128-poly1305 aegis-128l aegis-256 aez-384 deoxys-ii-256-128 rc4-md5 none

docs.title: password
docs.text: Shadowsocks password.

docs.title: udp-over-tcp
docs.text: Enables UDP over TCP. Default: false.

docs.title: udp-over-tcp-version
docs.text: Protocol version for UDP over TCP. Default: 1. Available values: 1/2.

docs.title: plugin
docs.text: Plugin. Supports obfs/v2ray-plugin/gost-plugin/shadow-tls/restls/kcptun/jls.

docs.title: plugin-opts
docs.text:
Plugin settings.

obfsv2ray-plugingost-pluginshadow-tlsrestlskcptunjls 
plugin: obfs
plugin-opts:
  mode: tls
  host: bing.com

  plugin: v2ray-plugin
  plugin-opts:
    mode: websocket # no QUIC now
      # tls: true # wss
      # You can get this with openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256 -inform pem -in yourcert.pem
      # Configuring a fingerprint enables SSL pinning
      # fingerprint: xxxx
      # skip-cert-verify: true
      # name-cert-verify: example.com
      # host: bing.com
      # path: "/"
      # mux: true
      # headers:
      #   custom: value
      # v2ray-http-upgrade: false

  plugin: gost-plugin
  plugin-opts:
    mode: websocket
      # tls: true # wss
      # You can get this with openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256 -inform pem -in yourcert.pem
      # Configuring a fingerprint enables SSL pinning
      # fingerprint: xxxx
      # skip-cert-verify: true
      # name-cert-verify: example.com
      # host: bing.com
      # path: "/"
      # mux: true
      # headers:
      #   custom: value

  plugin: shadow-tls
  client-fingerprint: chrome
  plugin-opts:
    host: "cloud.tencent.com"
    password: "shadow_tls_password"
    version: 2 # support 1/2/3

  plugin: restls
  client-fingerprint: chrome  # one of chrome, ios, firefox, safari
  plugin-opts:
    host: "www.microsoft.com" # should be a TLS 1.3 server
    password: [YOUR_RESTLS_PASSWORD]
    version-hint: "tls13"
    # Control your post-handshake traffic through restls-script
    # Hide proxy behaviors like "tls in tls".
    # see https://github.com/3andne/restls/blob/main/Restls-Script:%20Hide%20Your%20Proxy%20Traffic%20Behavior.md
    restls-script: "300?100<1,400~100,350~100,600~100,300~200,300~100"

  plugin: kcptun
  plugin-opts:
    key: it's a secrect # pre-shared secret between client and server
    crypt: aes # aes, aes-128, aes-128-gcm, aes-192, salsa20, blowfish, twofish, cast5, 3des, tea, xtea, xor, none, null
    mode: fast # profiles: fast3, fast2, fast, normal, manual
    conn: 1 # set num of UDP connections to server
    autoexpire: 0 # set auto expiration time(in seconds) for a single UDP connection, 0 to disable
    scavengettl: 600 # set how long an expired connection can live (in seconds)
    mtu: 1350 # set maximum transmission unit for UDP packets
    ratelimit: 0 # set maximum outgoing speed (in bytes per second) for a single KCP connection, 0 to disable. Also known as packet pacing
    sndwnd: 128 # set send window size(num of packets)
    rcvwnd: 512 # set receive window size(num of packets)
    datashard: 10 # set reed-solomon erasure coding - datashard
    parityshard: 3 # set reed-solomon erasure coding - parityshard
    dscp: 0 # set DSCP(6bit)
    nocomp: false # disable compression
    acknodelay: false # flush ack immediately when a packet is received
    nodelay: 0
    interval: 50
    resend: 0
    sockbuf: 4194304 # per-socket buffer in bytes
    smuxver: 1 # specify smux version, available 1,2
    smuxbuf: 4194304 # the overall de-mux buffer in bytes
    framesize: 8192 # smux max frame size
    streambuf: 2097152 # per stream receive buffer in bytes, smux v2+
    keepalive: 10 # seconds between heartbeats

plugin: jls
client-fingerprint: chrome
plugin-opts:
  host: "www.example.com"
  username: "jls-user"
  password: "jls-password"
  # alpn: [h2, http/1.1]

docs.title: SSH
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "ssh-out"
  type: ssh

  server: 127.0.0.1
  port: 22
  username: root
  password: password
  private-key: key
  private-key-passphrase: key_password
  host-key:
  - "ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAA..."
  host-key-algorithms:
  - rsa

Common fields

docs.title: username
docs.text: SSH user.

docs.title: password
docs.text: SSH password.

docs.title: private-key
docs.text: Key content or path.

docs.title: private_key_passphrase
docs.text: Key passphrase.

docs.title: host-key
docs.text: Host key. Leave empty to accept all.

docs.title: host-key-algorithms
docs.text: Host key algorithms.

docs.title: ShadowsocksR
docs.text:
proxies:
  - name: "ssr"
    type: ssr
    server: server
    port: 443
    cipher: chacha20-ietf
    password: "password"
    obfs: tls1.2_ticket_auth
    protocol: auth_sha1_v4
    # obfs-param: domain.tld
    # protocol-param: "#"
    # udp: true

Common fields

docs.title: Sudoku
docs.text:
proxies:
  - name: sudoku
    type: sudoku
    server: 1.2.3.4
    port: 443 
    key: "<client_key>"
    aead-method: chacha20-poly1305
    padding-min: 2
    padding-max: 7
    table-type: prefer_ascii
    # custom-table: xpxvvpvv
    # custom-tables: ["xpxvvpvv", "vxpvxvvp"]
    # multiplex: "off"
    httpmask:
      disable: false
      mode: legacy
      tls: true
      host: ""
      path-root: ""
      multiplex: "off"
    enable-pure-downlink: false

Common fields

docs.title: key
docs.text: If you use an ED25519 key pair generated by sudoku, fill in the private key from that key pair. Otherwise, fill in the same UUID as the server.

docs.title: aead-method
docs.text: Available values: chacha20-poly1305, aes-128-gcm, and none. Using none is discouraged because it provides no AEAD protection.

docs.title: padding-min
docs.text: Minimum padding ratio, from 0 to 100.

docs.title: padding-max
docs.text: Maximum padding ratio, from 0 to 100. It must be greater than or equal to padding-min.

docs.title: table-type
docs.text: Available values: prefer_ascii, prefer_entropy, up_ascii_down_entropy, up_entropy_down_ascii.

docs.title: custom-table
docs.text: Optional custom byte layout. It must contain 2 x, 2 p, and 4 v characters in any order. It only takes effect for the entropy direction.

docs.title: custom-tables
docs.text: Optional list of custom byte layouts (x/v/p). If non-empty, it overrides custom-table.

docs.title: multiplex
docs.text: Optional: off (default), auto (reuse only the underlying HTTPMask connection), on (enable Sudoku single-session multi-target mux over raw TCP or HTTPMask).

docs.title: httpmask.disable
docs.text: Whether to disable all HTTP masking/tunneling.

docs.title: httpmask.mode
docs.text: Optional: legacy (default), stream, poll, auto, ws. stream/poll/auto/ws support CDN or reverse proxy usage.

docs.title: httpmask.tls
docs.text: Optional. Only takes effect when mode is stream/poll/auto/ws. true forces HTTPS; false forces HTTP and does not infer from the port.

docs.title: httpmask.host
docs.text: Optional. Overrides Host/SNI and supports example.com or example.com:443. Only takes effect when mode is stream/poll/auto/ws.

docs.title: httpmask.path-root
docs.text: Optional. First-level path prefix for the HTTP tunnel endpoint. Both sides must match. For example, aabbcc maps to /aabbcc/session, /aabbcc/stream, /aabbcc/api/v1/upload, and /aabbcc/ws.

docs.title: httpmask.multiplex
docs.text: Legacy-compatible setting. If set, it takes precedence over the top-level multiplex.

docs.title: enable-pure-downlink
docs.text: Selects the downlink mode: false uses bandwidth-optimized downlink, while true uses pure Sudoku downlink. This setting must match the server.

docs.title: Tailscale
docs.text:
proxies:
  - name: "tailscale"
    type: tailscale
    hostname: mihomo
    auth-key: tskey-auth-xxxx
    control-url: https://controlplane.tailscale.com
    state-dir: ./tailscale
    ephemeral: false
    udp: true
    accept-routes: true
    exit-node: 100.64.0.1
    exit-node-allow-lan-access: true
    dialer-proxy: "ss1"
    interface-name: "WLAN"
    routing-mark: 6666
    ip-version: ipv4-prefer

Note

Like other proxy protocols, Tailscale is an optional proxy node. Traffic will only go through Tailscale if you direct it via rules, policy groups, or other routing methods.

Furthermore, all types of proxies only start connecting when the first matching connection is triggered (i.e., they do not start synchronously with Mihomo). Therefore, a timeout on the first attempt to access the Tailscale node is normal; please try again until Tailscale loads successfully.

docs.title: name
docs.text: Required, proxy name. Must be unique.

docs.title: type
docs.text: Required, fixed to tailscale.

docs.title: hostname
docs.text: Optional, Tailscale device name. If omitted, it is handled by tsnet.

docs.title: auth-key
docs.text: Optional, login key for Tailscale or Headscale. If omitted, an interactive login URL is printed in the logs on first startup.

docs.title: control-url
docs.text: Optional, custom Tailscale control server or Headscale URL.

docs.title: state-dir
docs.text: Optional, tsnet state directory. Default: tailscale.

docs.title: ephemeral
docs.text: Optional, whether to log in as an ephemeral node. Default: false.

docs.title: udp
docs.text: Optional, whether to enable UDP. Default: false.

docs.title: accept-routes
docs.text: Optional, whether to accept subnet routes published in the Tailnet.

docs.title: exit-node
docs.text:
Optional, use the specified exit node. You can specify a node IP, and auto:any is also supported.

When the target is not within Tailscale routes, the connection fails directly and does not fall back to direct. To access the public internet, configure an available exit-node, or accept subnet routes that cover the target network.

docs.title: exit-node-allow-lan-access
docs.text: Optional, whether to allow access to the local LAN when using an exit node.

docs.title: dialer-proxy
docs.text: Optional, identifier of an outbound proxy. When non-empty, the specified proxy is used for Tailscale control plane, DERP/STUN, and other service connections.

docs.title: interface-name
docs.text: Optional, specifies the outbound network interface.

docs.title: routing-mark
docs.text: Optional, configures fwmark on Linux.

docs.title: ip-version
docs.text:
Optional, specifies the IP version used for outbound connections.

Available values: dual/ipv4/ipv6/ipv4-prefer/ipv6-prefer.

docs.title: TLS Configuration
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "tls-example"
  tls: true
  sni: example.com
  servername: example.com
  fingerprint: xxx
  alpn:
  - h2
  - http/1.1
  skip-cert-verify: true
  # name-cert-verify: example.com
  # certificate: xxxx
  # private-key: xxx
  client-fingerprint: chrome
  reality-opts:
    public-key: xxxx
    short-id: xxxx
    support-x25519mlkem768: true
  ech-opts:
    enable: true
    config: base64_encoded_config
    # query-server-name: xxx.com
  shadow-tls-opts: 
    version: 3 
    password: shadow-tls-password
  restls-opts:
    password: restls-password
    version-hint: tls13
  jls-opts:
    username: jls-user
    password: jls-password
  tlsmirror-opts:
    primary-key: MDEyMzQ1Njc4OWFiY2RlZjAxMjM0NTY3ODlhYmNkZWY=
    explicit-nonce-ciphersuites: [
      156, 157, 158, 159, 160, 161, 162, 163, 164, 165, 166, 167, 168, 169, 170, 171,
      172, 173, 49195, 49196, 49197, 49198, 49199, 49200, 49201, 49202, 49290, 49291,
      49293, 49316, 49317, 49318, 49319, 49320, 49321, 49322, 49323, 49324, 49325,
      49326, 49327, 52392, 52393, 52394, 52395, 52396, 52397, 52398
    ]
    defer-instance-derived-write-time:
      base-nanoseconds: 0
      uniform-random-multiplier-nanoseconds: 0
    transport-layer-padding:
      enabled: false
    connection-enrolment:
      primary-egress-outbound: ""
    sequence-watermarking-enabled: false
    embedded-traffic-generator:
      steps:
        - name: example
          host: example.com
          path: /
          method: GET
          headers:
            - name: User-Agent
              value: mihomo
            - name: Accept
              values:
                - text/html
                - application/xhtml+xml
          connection-ready: true
          connection-recall-exit: true
          h2-do-not-wait-for-download-finish: false
          wait-time:
            base-nanoseconds: 1000000000
            uniform-random-multiplier-nanoseconds: 0
          next-step:
            - weight: 1
              goto-location: 0

docs.title: tls
docs.text: Enables TLS, applicable only to protocols that use tls, with the trojan protocol requiring it to be enabled.

docs.title: sni/servername
docs.text: The server name indication, referred to as servername in VMess/VLESS. If left empty, it defaults to the address in server.

docs.title: fingerprint
docs.text:
Certificate fingerprint, applicable only to protocols that use tls. You can obtain the fingerprint using the following command: 

openssl x509 -noout -fingerprint -sha256 -inform pem -in yourcert.pem
Alternatively, you can obtain it through the "Certificates" section of the "SHA256 Fingerprint" in Chrome's "Certificate Viewer".

Warning

  • When a leaf certificate (i.e., a certificate containing an SNI name) is entered, only the fingerprint of the certificate sent by the server is verified; no additional checks are performed.

  • When the fingerprint of other types of certificates (such as intermediate or root certificates) is entered, it will be verified whether the certificate chain sent by the server was issued by that certificate. From v1.19.20 onwards, the SNI/servername requirement must also be met.

  • The fingerprint in this field is the fingerprint of the complete certificate, not the "certificate public key fingerprint" defined in HPKP. Please do not confuse them.

docs.title: alpn
docs.text:
List of supported Application Layer Protocol Negotiation options, arranged in order of priority.

If both peers support ALPN, the selected protocol will be one from this list; if there are no mutually supported protocols, the connection will fail.

Refer to Application-Layer Protocol Negotiation

docs.title: skip-cert-verify
docs.text: Bypasses certificate verification, applicable only to protocols that use tls.

docs.title: name-cert-verify
docs.text: Optional. Only modifies the certificate's DNSName verification target, without altering the SNI.

docs.title: certificate
docs.text: If filled, this enables mTLS (must be filled in with private-key). The content is the certificate in PEM format or the path to the certificate.

docs.title: private-key
docs.text: If filled, this enables mTLS (must be filled in with certificate). The content is the private key corresponding to the certificate in PEM format or the path to the private key.

docs.title: client-fingerprint
docs.text:
Client uTLS fingerprint, applicable only to VMess/VLESS/Trojan/AnyTLS protocols.

Note

Options: chrome, firefox, safari, iOS, android, edge, 360, qq, random If random is selected, a modern browser fingerprint will be generated based on Cloudflare Radar data.

docs.title: reality-opts
docs.text:
Configuration for reality; if not empty, reality will be enabled.

Warning

Due to xray-core's deliberately incompatible behavior, we will not consider compatibility with xray v26.7.11+ versions. If it cannot be used, replace the server (such as mihomo native listener, sing-box, or an older xray-core version) or use another protocol instead.

docs.title: reality-opts.public-key
docs.text: Public key corresponding to the reality server's private key.

docs.title: reality-opts.short-id
docs.text: One of the server's short IDs.

docs.title: reality-opts.support-x25519mlkem768
docs.text: Support for X25519-MLKEM768 key exchange.

docs.title: ech-opts.enable
docs.text: Enables ECH (Encrypted Client Hello). Setting it to true enables ECH.

docs.title: ech-opts.config
docs.text:
The ECH configuration, if empty, will be resolved via DNS; otherwise, it will be specified by this value, in the format of base64 encoded ech parameters (For example, AEn+DQBFKwAgACABWIHUGj4u+PIggYXcR5JF0gYk3dCRioBW8uJq9H4mKAAIAAEAAQABAANAEnB1YmxpYy50bHMtZWNoLmRldgAA).

Info

You can use the command mihomo generate ech-keypair test.com to generate a compliant self-signed ECH configuration pair for both the server and client. Please replace test.com with the SNI domain name you want to expose. The content after Config: in the output can be filled here, and the content after Key: should be filled in the server-side ECH configuration (ech-key in mihomo's listeners).

docs.title: ech-opts.query-server-name
docs.text: Optional, if not empty, it is used to specify the domain name when resolving via DNS.

docs.title: shadow-tls-opts
docs.text: Requires tls: true. Uses sni / servername from the general configuration as the ShadowTLS SNI.

docs.title: shadow-tls-opts.version
docs.text: Supports v1 / v2 / v3. Defaults to v2 if left empty.

docs.title: shadow-tls-opts.password
docs.text: ShadowTLS password.

docs.title: restls-opts
docs.text: Requires tls: true. Uses sni / servername from the general configuration as the Restls SNI.

docs.title: restls-opts.password
docs.text: Restls password.

docs.title: restls-opts.version-hint
docs.text: TLS version hint. Available values: tls12 / tls13.

docs.title: restls-opts.restls-script
docs.text: Optional Restls carrier traffic script after the handshake.

docs.title: jls-opts
docs.text: Requires tls: true. Uses sni / servername from the general configuration as the JLS SNI.

docs.title: jls-opts.username
docs.text: JLS username.

docs.title: jls-opts.password
docs.text: JLS password.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts
docs.text:
When tls is true, configuring tlsmirror-opts enables tlsmirror. The TLS carrier used by tlsmirror uses servername, alpn, skip-cert-verify, name-cert-verify, fingerprint, certificate, private-key, client-fingerprint, and ech-opts from the same outbound. If servername is empty, server is used.

Note

Currently, only VMess supports enabling tlsmirror. Do not use it with other protocols.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.primary-key
docs.text: Required, base64 encoding of the 32-byte primary key.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.explicit-nonce-ciphersuites
docs.text: Cipher suites with explicit nonce used by the TLS 1.2 carrier.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.defer-instance-derived-write-time
docs.text: Delay before the first write.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.transport-layer-padding
docs.text: Transport-layer padding settings.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.connection-enrolment
docs.text: v2ray-compatible connection enrolment settings. For mihomo VMess outbound, primary-egress-outbound is usually left empty.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.connection-enrolment.primary-ingress-outbound
docs.text: Inbound-side control outbound tag used for connection enrolment. Usually used to match v2ray-compatible server configuration.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.connection-enrolment.primary-egress-outbound
docs.text: Outbound-side control outbound tag used for connection enrolment. For mihomo VMess outbound, this is usually left empty; v2ray can set a dedicated control outbound tag.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.sequence-watermarking-enabled
docs.text: Whether to enable sequence watermarking.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator
docs.text: Generates extra HTTP carrier traffic. The protocol is determined by alpn. It is disabled when steps is not configured.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps
docs.text: List of HTTP carrier traffic steps. Steps run in order unless next-step selects another step.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.name
docs.text: Step name, used only as an identifier.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.host
docs.text: HTTP request target host.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.path
docs.text: HTTP request path.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.method
docs.text: HTTP request method.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.headers
docs.text: HTTP request header list. Each item uses name for the header name, value for a single value, or values for multiple values.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.connection-ready
docs.text: Deliver the proxy connection after this step finishes.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.connection-recall-exit
docs.text: Exit the carrier traffic after the proxy connection is closed.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.h2-do-not-wait-for-download-finish
docs.text: When the carrier protocol is h2, do not wait for the response body to finish reading.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.wait-time
docs.text: Wait time after the step finishes. Fields are the same as tlsmirror-opts.defer-instance-derived-write-time.

docs.title: tlsmirror-opts.embedded-traffic-generator.steps.next-step
docs.text: Weighted candidate list for the next step. weight is the weight, and goto-location is the target step index.

docs.title: Transport Configuration
docs.text:
httph2grpcwsmkcpmekyaxhttp 
proxies:
- name: "http-opts-example"
  type: xxxxx
  network: http
  http-opts:
    method: "GET"
    path:
    - '/'
    - '/video'
    headers:
      Connection:
      - keep-alive

proxies:
- name: "h2-opts-example"
  type: xxxxx
  network: h2
  h2-opts:
    host:
    - example.com
    path: /

proxies:
- name: "grpc-opts-example"
  type: xxxxx
  network: grpc
  grpc-opts:
    grpc-service-name: example
    # grpc-user-agent:
    # ping-interval: 0
    # max-connections: 1
    # min-streams: 0
    # max-streams: 0

proxies:
- name: "ws-opts-example"
  type: xxxxx
  network: ws
  ws-opts:
    path: /path
    headers:
      Host: example.com
    max-early-data:
    early-data-header-name:
    v2ray-http-upgrade: false
    v2ray-http-upgrade-fast-open: false

proxies:
- name: "mkcp-opts-example"
  type: vmess
  server: server
  port: 443
  uuid: uuid
  alterId: 32
  cipher: auto
  network: mkcp
  mkcp-opts:
    mtu: 1350
    tti: 50
    uplink-capacity: 5
    downlink-capacity: 20
    congestion: false
    write-buffer: 2097152
    read-buffer: 2097152
    seed: ""
    header: ""

proxies:
- name: "mekya-opts-example"
  type: vmess
  server: server
  port: 443
  uuid: uuid
  alterId: 32
  cipher: auto
  tls: true
  network: mekya
  mekya-opts:
    url: https://server:443/mekya
    max-write-delay: 80
    max-request-size: 96000
    polling-interval-initial: 200
    h2-pool-size: 8
    kcp:
      mtu: 1350
      tti: 15
      uplink-capacity: 40
      downlink-capacity: 2000
      congestion: false
      write-buffer: 67108864
      read-buffer: 67108864
      seed: ""
      header: ""

proxies:
- name: "xhttp-opts-example"
  type: vless
  server: server
  port: 443
  uuid: uuid
  udp: true
  tls: true
  network: xhttp
  alpn: [h2] # By default, only h2 mode is supported. To enable h3 mode, you need to set alpn: [h3]; to enable HTTP/1.1 mode, you need to set alpn: [http/1.1].
  # ech-opts: ...
  # reality-opts: ...
  # skip-cert-verify: false
  # name-cert-verify: example.com
  # fingerprint: ...
  # certificate: ...
  # private-key: ...
  servername: xxx.com
  client-fingerprint: chrome
  encryption: ""
  xhttp-opts:
    path: "/"
    host: xxx.com
    # mode: "stream-one" # Available: "stream-one", "stream-up" or "packet-up"
    # headers:
    #   X-Forwarded-For: ""
    # no-grpc-header: false
    # x-padding-bytes: "100-1000"
    # x-padding-obfs-mode: false
    # x-padding-key: x_padding
    # x-padding-header: Referer
    # x-padding-placement: queryInHeader # Available: queryInHeader, cookie, header, query
    # x-padding-method: repeat-x # Available: repeat-x, tokenish
    # uplink-http-method: POST # Available: POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE
    # session-placement: path # Available: path, query, cookie, header
    # session-key: ""
    # session-table: "" # Available: "", "uuid", "ALPHABET", "Alphabet", "BASE36", "Base62", "HEX", "alphabet", "base36", "hex", "number"
    # session-length: "16-32"
    # seq-placement: path # Available: path, query, cookie, header
    # seq-key: ""
    # uplink-data-placement: body # Available: body, cookie, header
    # uplink-data-key: ""
    # uplink-chunk-size: 0 # only applicable when uplink-data-placement is not body
    # sc-max-each-post-bytes: 1000000
    # sc-min-posts-interval-ms: 30
    # reuse-settings: # aka XMUX
    #   max-concurrency: "16-32"
    #   max-connections: "0"
    #   c-max-reuse-times: "0"
    #   h-max-request-times: "600-900"
    #   h-max-reusable-secs: "1800-3000"
    #   h-keep-alive-period: 0
    # download-settings:
    #   ## xhttp part
    #   path: "/"
    #   host: xxx.com
    #   headers:
    #     X-Forwarded-For: ""
    #   reuse-settings: # aka XMUX
    #     max-concurrency: "16-32"
    #     max-connections: "0"
    #     c-max-reuse-times: "0"
    #     h-max-request-times: "600-900"
    #     h-max-reusable-secs: "1800-3000"
    #     h-keep-alive-period: 0
    #   ## proxy part
    #   server: server
    #   port: 443
    #   tls: true
    #   alpn: ...
    #   ech-opts: ...
    #   shadow-tls-opts: ...
    #   restls-opts: ...
    #   jls-opts: ...
    #   reality-opts: ...
    #   skip-cert-verify: false
    #   name-cert-verify: example.com
    #   fingerprint: ...
    #   certificate: ...
    #   private-key: ...
    #   servername: xxx.com
    #   client-fingerprint: chrome

docs.title: http-opts
docs.text: http transport settings. Only effective when the transport layer is http.

docs.title: http-opts.method
docs.text: HTTP request method.

docs.title: http-opts.path
docs.text: HTTP request path.

docs.title: http-opts.headers
docs.text:
HTTP request headers.

Note

Mihomo's H2 transport layer does not implement multiplexing. If multiplexing is needed, gRPC or sing-mux is recommended in Mihomo.

docs.title: h2-opts
docs.text: h2 transport settings. Only effective when the transport layer is h2.

docs.title: h2-opts.host
docs.text: List of hostnames. If set, the client randomly selects one and the server validates it.

docs.title: h2-opts.path
docs.text: HTTP request path.

docs.title: grpc-opts
docs.text: grpc transport settings. Only effective when the transport layer is grpc.

docs.title: grpc-opts.grpc-service-name
docs.text: gRPC service name.

docs.title: grpc-opts.grpc-user-agent
docs.text: gRPC User-Agent.

docs.title: grpc-opts.ping-interval
docs.text: gRPC heartbeat interval. Disabled by default, in seconds.

docs.title: grpc-opts.max-connections
docs.text:
Maximum number of connections. The default value is 1, meaning only one underlying connection is used.

Conflicts with max-streams.

docs.title: grpc-opts.min-streams
docs.text:
Minimum number of multiplexed streams in a connection before opening a new connection.

Conflicts with max-streams.

docs.title: grpc-opts.max-streams
docs.text:
Maximum number of multiplexed streams in a connection before opening a new connection.

Conflicts with max-connections and min-streams.

docs.title: ws-opts
docs.text: ws transport settings. Only effective when the transport layer is ws.

docs.title: ws-opts.path
docs.text: Request path.

docs.title: ws-opts.headers
docs.text: Request headers.

docs.title: ws-opts.max-early-data
docs.text: Early Data first-packet length threshold.

docs.title: ws-opts.v2ray-http-upgrade
docs.text: Use HTTP upgrade.

docs.title: ws-opts.v2ray-http-upgrade-fast-open
docs.text: Enable fast open for HTTP upgrade.

docs.title: mkcp-opts
docs.text:
mkcp transport settings. Only effective when the transport layer is mkcp.

Note

Only VMess supports the mKCP transport layer. Do not use it with other protocols.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.mtu
docs.text: Maximum transmission unit.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.tti
docs.text: Transmission time interval in milliseconds.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.uplink-capacity
docs.text: Uplink capacity in MB/s.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.downlink-capacity
docs.text: Downlink capacity in MB/s.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.congestion
docs.text: Whether to enable congestion control.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.write-buffer
docs.text: Write buffer size in bytes.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.read-buffer
docs.text: Read buffer size in bytes.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.seed
docs.text: Seed used for AES-GCM authentication. Leave empty to use the default authentication.

docs.title: mkcp-opts.header
docs.text: Packet header camouflage. Available values: none/srtp/utp/wechat-video/dtls/wireguard.

docs.title: mekya-opts
docs.text:
mekya transport settings. Only effective when the transport layer is mekya.

Note

Only VMess supports the Mekya transport layer. Do not use it with other protocols.

docs.title: mekya-opts.url
docs.text: Mekya server URL.

docs.title: mekya-opts.max-write-delay
docs.text: Maximum aggregation wait time after the first packet, in milliseconds.

docs.title: mekya-opts.max-request-size
docs.text: Maximum payload size of a single HTTP request, in bytes.

docs.title: mekya-opts.polling-interval-initial
docs.text: Empty polling interval in milliseconds.

docs.title: mekya-opts.h2-pool-size
docs.text: HTTP/2 connection pool size.

docs.title: mekya-opts.kcp
docs.text: Internal KCP parameters for Mekya. The fields are the same as mkcp-opts.

docs.title: xhttp-opts
docs.text:
xhttp transport settings. Only effective when the transport layer is xhttp.

By default, only h2 is supported. To enable h3 mode, set alpn: [h3]. To enable HTTP/1.1 mode, set alpn: [http/1.1].

Note

Only VLESS supports the xhttp transport layer. Do not use it with other protocols.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.path
docs.text: Request path.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.host
docs.text: Hostname.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.mode
docs.text: Mode. Available values: auto, stream-one, stream-up, or packet-up.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.headers
docs.text: Request headers.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.no-grpc-header
docs.text: Sets whether stream-up/one uplink sends a Content-Type: application/grpc header to mimic gRPC.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.x-padding-bytes
docs.text: The request headers sent by the client include Referer: ...?x_padding=XXX... by default. The default length is 100-1000 and is randomized for each request. The server checks whether it falls within the allowed range.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.x-padding-obfs-mode
docs.text: Enables padding obfuscation. Defaults to false for backward compatibility.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.x-padding-key
docs.text:
Key name used to store the padding value. Its meaning depends on x-padding-placement:

  • Query parameter name in the URL when placement is queryInHeader.
  • Cookie name.
  • HTTP header name.
  • URL query parameter name.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.x-padding-header
docs.text: HTTP header name. Only relevant when x-padding-placement is header or queryInHeader.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.x-padding-placement
docs.text: Defines where the padding value is placed. Available values: queryInHeader, cookie, header, query. Only valid when x-padding-obfs-mode is true.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.x-padding-method
docs.text:
Defines how the padding value is generated.

  • repeat-x: default method, a long sequence containing X characters.
  • tokenish: generates a random Base62 token.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.uplink-http-method
docs.text: Changes the HTTP method used for uplink data transfer. Use any method that supports a request body, such as PUT or PATCH, and is allowed by the CDN.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.session-placement
docs.text: Placement of the session ID. Options: path, query, cookie, header.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.session-key
docs.text: Key name for the session ID. Not applicable when placement is path.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.session-table
docs.text: Character table used to generate session IDs. Available values: "", uuid, ALPHABET, Alphabet, BASE36, Base62, HEX, alphabet, base36, hex, number.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.session-length
docs.text: Session ID length range. The start value cannot be 0; the total ID space must be larger than 2.1 billion. Effective only when session-table is non-empty or uuid.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.seq-placement
docs.text: Placement of the sequence number. Options: path, query, cookie, header. If session-placement is set to path, seq-placement must also be set to path.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.seq-key
docs.text: Key name for the sequence number. Not applicable when placement is path.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.uplink-data-placement
docs.text: Placement of split uplink data fragments, only for packet-up mode. Options: cookie or header.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.uplink-data-key
docs.text: Base name of the key used to pass data fragments. The client automatically splits data into multiple chunks and the server reassembles them.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.uplink-chunk-size
docs.text:
Maximum size of each uplink data chunk, in bytes. Only applicable when uplink-data-placement is not body.

Minimum value: 64 bytes.

Recommended values:

  • For cookie placement: 3072 bytes (default 3KB).
  • For header placement: 4096 bytes (default 4KB).

If unspecified, an appropriate default is selected automatically based on uplink-data-placement.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.sc-max-each-post-bytes
docs.text: Maximum amount of data carried by each client POST. Default: 1000000, or 1MB. This value should be smaller than the maximum allowed by CDNs and other HTTP middleboxes. The server also rejects POSTs larger than this value.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.sc-min-posts-interval-ms
docs.text: Minimum interval for the client to initiate POST requests based on a single proxy request. Default: 30 milliseconds.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.reuse-settings
docs.text:
Connection reuse settings, also known as XMUX.

Note: unlike the original implementation, this option has no default value. If omitted, connection reuse is disabled and each request opens a new underlying connection.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.reuse-settings.max-concurrency
docs.text: Maximum number of proxy requests that can exist simultaneously in each underlying connection. When the number of proxy requests in the connection reaches this value, a new connection is established to accommodate more requests.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.reuse-settings.max-connections
docs.text:
Maximum number of simultaneous connections. Before this value is reached, each new proxy request opens a new connection. After that, existing connections start to be reused.

This conflicts with max-concurrency; only one can be used.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.reuse-settings.c-max-reuse-times
docs.text: Maximum number of times a connection can be reused. After this count is reached, it will not be assigned new proxy requests and will be closed after the last internal proxy request finishes.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.reuse-settings.h-max-request-times
docs.text: Maximum cumulative number of requests carried by a connection. This counter is not strict, and Golang GET requests have automatic retries, so filling this is not recommended.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.reuse-settings.h-max-reusable-secs
docs.text: After a TCP/QUIC connection lasts this long, it will no longer be assigned new HTTP requests and will be closed after the last internal HTTP request finishes.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.reuse-settings.h-keep-alive-period
docs.text: How often the client sends keepalive packets when the H2/H3 connection is idle. Default: 0, meaning Chrome H2's 45 seconds or quic-go H3's 10 seconds. This is the only XMUX option that does not allow ranges, because randomizing it is itself a fingerprint, and it allows negative values such as -1 to disable idle keepalive packets. Leaving it at 0 is recommended.

docs.title: xhttp-opts.download-settings
docs.text:
Separate upload/download settings.

Note: this option overrides the original configuration. Any item left unset inherits the uplink parameter. Only the options listed in the example can be overridden; unlisted options are not overwritten.

docs.title: Trojan
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "trojan"
  type: trojan
  server: server
  port: 443
  password: yourpsk
  udp: true

  sni: example.com
  alpn:
  - h2
  - http/1.1
  client-fingerprint: random
  fingerprint: xxxx
  skip-cert-verify: true
  name-cert-verify: example.com
  shadow-tls-opts:
    version: 3
    password: shadow-tls-password
  restls-opts:
    password: restls-password
    version-hint: tls13
  jls-opts:
    username: jls-user
    password: jls-password
  ss-opts:
    enabled: false
    method: aes-128-gcm
    password: "example"
  reality-opts:
    public-key: xxxx
    short-id: xxxx

  network: tcp

  smux:
    enabled: false

Common fields

TLS fields

docs.title: password
docs.text: Required, trojan server password.

docs.title: ss-opfs.enabled
docs.text: Enables trojan-go Shadowsocks AEAD encryption.

docs.title: ss-opfs.method
docs.text: Encryption method. Supports aes-128-gcm/aes-256-gcm/chacha20-ietf-poly1305.

docs.title: ss-opfs.password
docs.text: trojan-go Shadowsocks AEAD encryption password.

docs.title: network
docs.text:
Transport layer. Supports ws/grpc. If unset or set to another value, TCP is used.

See Transport configuration.

docs.title: TrustTunnel
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: trusttunnel
  type: trusttunnel
  server: 1.2.3.4
  port: 443
  username: username
  password: password
  # client-fingerprint: chrome
  health-check: true
  udp: true
  # sni: "example.com"
  # alpn:
  #   - h2
  # skip-cert-verify: true
  # name-cert-verify: example.com
  ### quic options
  # quic: true
  # congestion-controller: bbr
  # bbr-profile: "" # Available: "standard", "conservative", "aggressive". Default: "standard"
  ### reuse options
  # max-connections: 8
  # min-streams: 5
  # max-streams: 0

Common fields

TLS fields

docs.title: quic
docs.text: Whether to enable QUIC. Default: false.

docs.title: congestion-controller
docs.text: Sets the QUIC congestion control algorithm.

docs.title: max-connections
docs.text:
Maximum number of connections.

Conflicts with max-streams.

docs.title: min-streams
docs.text:
Minimum number of multiplexed streams in a connection before opening a new connection.

Conflicts with max-streams.

docs.title: max-streams
docs.text:
Maximum number of multiplexed streams in a connection before opening a new connection.

Conflicts with max-connections and min-streams.

docs.title: TUIC
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: tuic
  server: www.example.com
  port: 10443
  type: tuic
  token: TOKEN
  uuid: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
  password: PASSWORD_1
  # ip: 127.0.0.1
  # heartbeat-interval: 10000
  # alpn: [h3]
  disable-sni: true
  reduce-rtt: true
  request-timeout: 8000
  udp-relay-mode: native
  # congestion-controller: bbr
  # bbr-profile: "" # Available: "standard", "conservative", "aggressive". Default: "standard"
  # max-udp-relay-packet-size: 1500
  # fast-open: true
  # skip-cert-verify: true
  # name-cert-verify: example.com
  # max-open-streams: 20
  # sni: example.com

Common fields

TLS fields

docs.title: token
docs.text: Required, user identifier for TUIC V4. Must not be written when using TUIC V5.

docs.title: uuid
docs.text: Required, unique user identifier for TUIC V5. Must not be written when using TUIC V4.

docs.title: password
docs.text: Required, user password for TUIC V5. Must not be written when using TUIC V4.

docs.title: ip
docs.text: Overrides the DNS lookup result for the address set in the server option.

docs.title: heartbeat-interval
docs.text: Interval for sending keepalive heartbeat packets, in milliseconds.

docs.title: disable-sni
docs.text: Whether to disable SNI (Server Name Indication) in the TLS handshake. SNI is used to host multiple HTTPS sites on the same IP address.

docs.title: reduce-rtt
docs.text: Whether to enable QUIC 0-RTT handshakes on the client. This can reduce connection setup time, but may increase replay attack risk.

docs.title: request-timeout
docs.text: Timeout for establishing a connection to the TUIC proxy server, in milliseconds.

docs.title: udp-relay-mode
docs.text: UDP packet relay mode. Available values: native/quic.

docs.title: congestion-controller
docs.text: Congestion control algorithm. Available values: cubic/new_reno/bbr.

docs.title: max-udp-relay-packet-size
docs.text: Maximum UDP relay packet size, in bytes.

docs.title: fast-open
docs.text: Whether to enable Fast Open, which can reduce connection setup time.

docs.title: max-open-streams
docs.text: Maximum number of open streams. Too many open streams may affect performance.

docs.title: VLESS
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "vless"
  type: vless
  server: server
  port: 443
  udp: true
  uuid: uuid
  flow: xtls-rprx-vision
  packet-encoding: xudp

  tls: true
  servername: example.com
  alpn:
  - h2
  - http/1.1
  fingerprint: xxxx
  client-fingerprint: chrome
  skip-cert-verify: true
  name-cert-verify: example.com
  shadow-tls-opts:
    version: 3
    password: shadow-tls-password
  restls-opts:
    password: restls-password
    version-hint: tls13
  jls-opts:
    username: jls-user
    password: jls-password
  reality-opts:
    public-key: xxxx
    short-id: xxxx
  encryption: ""

  network: tcp

  smux:
    enabled: false

Note

Meta's xtls-* flow control is effectively equivalent to xtls-*-udp443 in Xray-core. To block UDP traffic on port 443, use the logical rule: AND,((NETWORK,UDP),(DST-PORT,443)),REJECT.

Common fields

TLS fields

docs.title: uuid
docs.text: Required, VLESS user ID.

docs.title: flow
docs.text: VLESS sub-protocol. Available value: xtls-rprx-vision.

docs.title: packet-encoding
docs.text: UDP packet encoding. Empty means raw encoding. Available values: packetaddr (supported by v2ray 5+)/ xudp (supported by xray).

docs.title: encryption
docs.text:
VLESS encryption client configuration:

encryption: "mlkem768x25519plus.native/xorpub/random.1rtt/0rtt.(padding len).(padding gap).(X25519 Password).(ML-KEM-768 Client)..."

XTLS Vision with native/xorpub can splice. Use only 1-RTT mode, or reuse 0-RTT if the ticket sent by the server has non-zero seconds.

Only one item can be selected before /; after that, at least one base64 item is required and more can be appended indefinitely. Generate values with mihomo generate vless-x25519 and mihomo generate vless-mlkem768, and remove the parentheses when replacing values.

Padding is optional and only applies to 1-RTT to remove handshake length characteristics. The default value on both sides is 100-111-1111.75-0-111.50-0-3333:

  • After the 1-RTT client/server hello, append random padding of 111 to 1111 bytes with 100% probability.

  • Wait a random 0 to 111 milliseconds with 75% probability (probability-from-to).

  • Send random padding of 0 to 3333 bytes again with 50% probability. If the value is 0, no Write() occurs.

  • The server and client can use different padding parameters. Parameters can be appended indefinitely in len/gap order. The first padding must have 100% probability and at least 35 bytes.

docs.title: network
docs.text:
Transport layer. Supports ws/http/h2/grpc/xhttp. If unset or set to another value, TCP is used.

See Transport configuration.

docs.title: VMess
docs.text:
proxies:
- name: "vmess"
  type: vmess
  server: server
  port: 443
  udp: true
  uuid: uuid
  alterId: 0
  cipher: auto
  packet-encoding: packetaddr
  global-padding: false
  authenticated-length: false

  tls: true
  servername: example.com
  alpn:
  - h2
  - http/1.1
  fingerprint: xxxx
  client-fingerprint: chrome
  skip-cert-verify: true
  name-cert-verify: example.com
  shadow-tls-opts:
    version: 3
    password: shadow-tls-password
  restls-opts:
    password: restls-password
    version-hint: tls13
  jls-opts:
    username: jls-user
    password: jls-password
  reality-opts:
    public-key: xxxx
    short-id: xxxx
  tlsmirror-opts:
    primary-key: xxxx

  network: tcp

  smux:
    enabled: false

Common fields

TLS fields

docs.title: uuid
docs.text: Required, VMess user ID.

docs.title: alterId
docs.text: Required. If not 0, the legacy protocol is enabled.

docs.title: cipher
docs.text: Required, encryption method. Supports auto/none/zero/aes-128-gcm/chacha20-poly1305.

docs.title: packet-encoding
docs.text: UDP packet encoding. Empty means raw encoding. Available values: packetaddr (supported by v2ray 5+)/xudp (supported by xray).

docs.title: global-padding
docs.text: Protocol parameter. If enabled, traffic is randomly wasted. It is enabled by default in v2ray and cannot be disabled there.

docs.title: authenticated-length
docs.text: Protocol parameter. Enables encrypted length blocks.

docs.title: network
docs.text:
Transport layer. Supports ws/http/h2/grpc/mkcp/mekya. If unset or set to another value, TCP is used.

See Transport configuration.

docs.title: Simplified syntax
docs.text:
If there is only one peer, you can use the simplified syntax.

proxies:
- name: "wg"
  type: wireguard
  private-key: eCtXsJZ27+4PbhDkHnB923tkUn2Gj59wZw5wFA75MnU=
  server: 162.159.192.1
  port: 2480
  ip: 172.16.0.2
  ipv6: fd01:5ca1:ab1e:80fa:ab85:6eea:213f:f4a5
  public-key: Cr8hWlKvtDt7nrvf+f0brNQQzabAqrjfBvas9pmowjo=
  allowed-ips: ['0.0.0.0/0']
  # pre-shared-key: 31aIhAPwktDGpH4JDhA8GNvjFXEf/a6+UaQRyOAiyfM=
  # reserved: [209,98,59]  # String format is also valid, such as "U4An"
  # persistent-keepalive: 0
  udp: true
  # mtu: 1408
  # dialer-proxy: "ss1"  # Identifier of an outbound proxy. When non-empty, connections are sent through the specified proxy/proxy-group
  # remote-dns-resolve: true # Force remote DNS resolution, default is false
  # dns: [ 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8 ] # Effective only when remote-dns-resolve is true
  # If present, enables AmneziaWG functionality
  # amnezia-wg-option:
  #   jc: 5
  #   jmin: 500
  #   jmax: 501
  #   s1: 30
  #   s2: 40
  #   s3: 50                                            # AmneziaWG v1.5 and v2
  #   s4: 5                                             # AmneziaWG v1.5 and v2
  #   h1: 123456                                        # AmneziaWG v1.0 and v1.5
  #   h2: 67543                                         # AmneziaWG v1.0 and v1.5
  #   h3: 123123                                        # AmneziaWG v1.0 and v1.5
  #   h4: 32345                                         # AmneziaWG v1.0 and v1.5
  #   h1: 123456-123500                                 # AmneziaWG v2.0 only
  #   h2: 67543-67550                                   # AmneziaWG v2.0 only
  #   h3: 123123-123200                                 # AmneziaWG v2.0 only
  #   h4: 32345-32350                                   # AmneziaWG v2.0 only
  #   i1: <b 0xf6ab3267fa><c><b 0xf6ab><t><r 10><wt 10> # AmneziaWG v1.5 and v2
  #   i2: <b 0xf6ab3267fa><r 100>                       # AmneziaWG v1.5 and v2
  #   i3: ""                                            # AmneziaWG v1.5 and v2
  #   i4: ""                                            # AmneziaWG v1.5 and v2
  #   i5: ""                                            # AmneziaWG v1.5 and v2
  #   j1: <b 0xffffffff><c><b 0xf6ab><t><r 10>          # AmneziaWG v1.5 only (removed in v2)
  #   j2: <c><b 0xf6ab><t><wt 1000>                     # AmneziaWG v1.5 only (removed in v2)
  #   j3: <t><b 0xf6ab><c><r 10>                        # AmneziaWG v1.5 only (removed in v2)
  #   itime: 60                                         # AmneziaWG v1.5 only (removed in v2)

docs.title: Full syntax
docs.text:
The full syntax can specify multiple peers.

When using multiple peers, each peer's allowed-ips needs to be distinct. In this case, top-level fields such as server, port, public-key, pre-shared-key, and reserved are ignored, but private-key is still specified at the top level.

proxies:
- name: "wg"
  type: wireguard
  ip: 172.16.0.2
  ipv6: fd01:5ca1:ab1e:80fa:ab85:6eea:213f:f4a5
  private-key: eCtXsJZ27+4PbhDkHnB923tkUn2Gj59wZw5wFA75MnU=
  peers:
    - server: 162.159.192.1
      port: 2480
      public-key: Cr8hWlKvtDt7nrvf+f0brNQQzabAqrjfBvas9pmowjo=
      allowed-ips: ['0.0.0.0/0']
      # pre-shared-key: 31aIhAPwktDGpH4JDhA8GNvjFXEf/a6+UaQRyOAiyfM=
      # reserved: [209,98,59]  # String format is also valid, such as "U4An"
  udp: true
  # mtu: 1408
  # dialer-proxy: "ss1"  # Identifier of an outbound proxy. When non-empty, connections are sent through the specified proxy/proxy-group
  # remote-dns-resolve: true # Force remote DNS resolution, default is false
  # dns: [ 1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8 ] # Effective only when remote-dns-resolve is true

Common fields

docs.title: ip
docs.text: IPv4 address used by the local machine in the WireGuard network.

docs.title: ipv6
docs.text: Optional field, IPv6 address used by the local machine in the WireGuard network.

docs.title: private-key
docs.text:
Base64-encoded WireGuard client private key.

You can use wg genkey | tee privatekey | wg pubkey > publickey to generate a usable public/private key pair.

docs.title: public-key
docs.text: Base64-encoded WireGuard server public key.

docs.title: allowed-ips
docs.text: Optional field, restricts which client IP ranges are forwarded by the server. Usually ['0.0.0.0/0'] can be used.

docs.title: pre-shared-key
docs.text: Optional field, pre-shared key.

docs.title: reserved
docs.text: Optional field, value of the WireGuard protocol reserved field. Required by some WARP nodes.

docs.title: persistent-keepalive
docs.text: Optional field, periodically sends packets to keep the connection persistent.

docs.title: mtu
docs.text: Optional field, sets the MTU value.

docs.title: remote-dns-resolve
docs.text: Optional field, whether to force remote DNS resolution. Default: false.

docs.title: dns
docs.text: Optional field. Takes effect when remote-dns-resolve is true and specifies DNS servers used for remote resolution.

docs.title: Translating from a standard WireGuard configuration file
docs.text:
Assume the following standard WireGuard configuration file:

[Interface]
Address = <local network IP>
ListenPort = <local listening port>
PrivateKey = <local private key>
DNS = <DNS to use>
MTU = <preset MTU>

[Peer]
AllowedIPs = <forwarded IP range>
Endpoint = <remote address>:<remote port>
PublicKey = <remote public key>

The corresponding clash node configuration is:

- name: "wg"
  type: wireguard
  ip: <local network IP, fill IPv4 here>
  ipv6: <local network IP, fill IPv6 here>  # Delete if there is no v6 address
  private-key: <local private key>
  peers:
    - server: <remote address>
      port: <remote port>
      public-key: <remote public key>
      allowed-ips: ['0.0.0.0/0']     # Traffic splitting is handled by clash
      # reserved: [209,98,59]        # Fill in if needed
  udp: true
  mtu: <preset MTU>               # Set as needed, delete if not needed
  remote-dns-resolve: true        # Set as needed, delete if not needed
  dns: <DNS to use>               # Set as needed, delete if not needed

docs.title: General Fields
docs.text:
proxy-groups:
- name: "proxy"
  type: select
  proxies:
  - DIRECT
  - ss
  use:
  - provider1
  - provider1

  url: 'https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204'
  interval: 300
  lazy: true
  default-selected: ss
  empty-fallback: COMPATIBLE
  timeout: 5000
  max-failed-times: 5

  disable-udp: true
  interface-name: en0
  routing-mark: 11451
  include-all: false
  include-all-proxies: false
  include-all-providers: false
  filter: "(?i)港|hk|hongkong|hong kong"
  exclude-filter: "美|日"
  exclude-type: "Shadowsocks|Http"
  expected-status: 204
  hidden: true
  icon: xxx

docs.title: name
docs.text:
Required, the name of the proxy group.

Note

If there are special characters, they should be enclosed in quotes.

docs.title: type
docs.text: Required, the type of the proxy group.

docs.title: proxies
docs.text: References to outbound proxies or other proxy groups.

docs.title: use
docs.text: References to proxy sets.

docs.title: url
docs.text:
Health check test address.

Note

It will only check the proxies field of the proxy group, and will not check the proxies of the proxy providers introduced through use.

docs.title: interval
docs.text: Health check interval; if not 0, periodic testing is enabled, measured in seconds.

docs.title: lazy
docs.text: Lazy state, defaults to true. If the current proxy group is not selected, no testing is performed.

docs.title: default-selected
docs.text: The node selected by default. If this field is empty or the node name does not exist, the first node in the group is selected.

docs.title: empty-fallback
docs.text:
The fallback proxy when the group is empty. The default value is COMPATIBLE.

Note

You cannot enter the proxy group here; only the proxy name is supported.

docs.title: timeout
docs.text: Health check timeout, measured in milliseconds.

docs.title: max-failed-times
docs.text: Maximum number of failures; exceeding this triggers a forced health check, default is 5.

docs.title: disable-udp
docs.text: Disables UDP for this proxy group.

docs.title: interface-name
docs.text:
Warning

The interface-name in the proxy group is deprecated, please use the interface-name in the proxy node.

Specifies the outbound interface for the proxy group.

Priority: Proxy Node > Proxy Policy > Global.

docs.title: routing-mark
docs.text:
Warning

The routing-mark in the proxy group is deprecated, please use the routing-mark in the proxy node.

The routing mark attached when the proxy group is outbound.

Priority: Proxy Node > Proxy Policy > Global.

docs.title: include-all
docs.text:
Includes all outbound proxies and proxy sets, sorted by name.

Inclusion does not include proxy groups; other proxy groups can be included in proxies.

docs.title: include-all-proxies
docs.text:
Includes all outbound proxies, sorted by name.

Inclusion does not include proxy groups; other proxy groups can be included in proxies.

docs.title: include-all-providers
docs.text:
Includes all proxy sets, sorted by name.

This will invalidate including proxy sets.

docs.title: filter
docs.text:
Filters nodes that match keywords or regular expressions. Use | to separate multiple regular expressions.

This only applies to included proxy sets and including all outbound proxies.

docs.title: exclude-filter
docs.text: Excludes nodes that match keywords or regular expressions. Use | to separate multiple regular expressions.

docs.title: exclude-type
docs.text:
Regular expressions are not supported. Split by | and exclude by node type. This applies only to included outbound proxies.

For supported types, please refer to Adapter Type, ignore case sensitivity

docs.title: expected-status
docs.text: The expected HTTP response status code during health checks. If this field is configured, a node is considered available only when the response status code matches the expected status. The default is *, indicating no requirements on the response status.

docs.title: syntax
docs.text: You can use / to match multiple status codes, - to match a range of statuses, and mix them.

docs.title: example
docs.text:
Match status codes 200 and 302.

expected-status: 200/302

Match status codes from 400 to 503.

expected-status: 400-503

Match status codes 200 and 302, as well as from 400 to 503.

expected-status: 200/302/400-503

docs.title: hidden
docs.text: Returns hidden status in the API to hide the display of this proxy group (requires front-end adaptation using the API).

docs.title: icon
docs.text: Returns the string input for icon in the API to display in this proxy group (requires front-end adaptation using the API).

docs.title: GLOBAL
docs.text:
Default fill all proxy-groups and proxies

The strategy group can also be customized in the configuration file

However, the web panel and some client applications sort according to the order of policy groups within the GLOBAL policy group

It is recommended to write out all the policy groups of the current configuration in full (except for the GLOBAL itself) when customizing the GLOBAL policy group

docs.title: Fallback
docs.text:
proxy-groups:
- name: "fallback"
  type: fallback
  proxies:
  - ss
  - vmess
  url: 'https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204'
  interval: 300
  #lazy: true

If the current node times out, the first available node will be selected in the order of proxies

docs.title: Common Fields
docs.text: Refer to Common Fields.

docs.title: Load-balance
docs.text:
proxy-groups:
- name: "load-balance"
  type: load-balance
  proxies:
  - ss1
  - ss2
  - vmess1
  url: 'https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204'
  interval: 300
  #lazy: true
  #strategy: consistent-hashing # or round-robin

docs.title: Common Fields
docs.text: Refer to Common Fields.

docs.title: strategy
docs.text:
Load Balancing Strategies

  • round-robin will distribute all requests among different proxy nodes within the strategy group.

  • consistent-hashing will assign requests with the same target address to the same proxy node within the strategy group.

  • sticky-sessions: requests with the same source address and target address will be directed to the same proxy node within the strategy group, with a cache expiration of 10 minutes.

Note

When the target address is a domain, it uses top-level domain matching.

docs.title: Relay
docs.text:
Warning

The relay strategy has been deprecated. Please use dialer-proxy.

docs.title: Common Fields
docs.text: Refer to Common Fields.

docs.title: Common Fields
docs.text: Refer to Common Fields.

docs.title: tolerance
docs.text: proxies switch tolerance, measured in milliseconds (ms).

docs.title: Proxy Providers
docs.text:
proxy-providers:
  provider1:
    type: http
    url: "http://test.com"
    path: ./proxy_providers/provider1.yaml
    interval: 3600
    proxy: DIRECT
    size-limit: 0
    age-secret-key: AGE-SECRET-KEY-1ZTQLLN0A4U3ZTT3DCZKYN0CGZEZQLWX2DFTXUWMT4ZHR0N2UG6LSW9NT0N
    header:
