docs.title: name
docs.text: Required. For example, provider1. It must be unique and it is recommended not to duplicate names with Proxy Groups.

docs.title: type
docs.text: Required. The type of the provider. Available options: http / file / inline.

docs.title: url
docs.text: Required if the type is set to http.

docs.title: path
docs.text:
Optional. The file path. It must be unique. If left empty, the MD5 hash of the URL will be used as the filename.

Due to security concerns, this path is restricted to the HomeDir (configured via the -d startup parameter) by default. If you wish to store it in another location, please specify additional safe paths by setting the SAFE_PATHS environment variable. The syntax of this environment variable follows the parsing rules of the host operating system's PATH variable (i.e., separated by semicolons on Windows, and by colons on other operating systems).

docs.title: interval
docs.text: The update interval for the provider, in seconds.

docs.title: proxy
docs.text: Downloads/updates through the specified proxy.

docs.title: size-limit
docs.text: Limits the maximum size of the downloaded file. Default is 0, which means no file size limit. The unit is bytes (b).

docs.title: age-secret-key
docs.text:
If configured, the core will attempt to decrypt the age armor-formatted configuration file using this secret key.

Note:

  • For encrypted content, only the official ASCII "armor" format of age-encryption.org/v1 is currently supported.
  • For the key format, only the x25519 recipient type of age-encryption.org/v1 and The mlkem768-x25519 hybrid post-quantum recipient type are currently supported.
  • Currently, the core will not actively send the public key to the server. Users need to manually set the X-Age-Public-Key in the header or upload the public key via other methods.
  • The core also supports loading encrypted configuration files via the -age-secret-key command-line parameter or the CLASH_AGE_SECRET_KEY environment variable.

Utilities:

  • You can generate a compliant x25519 key via mihomo age keygen.
  • You can generate a compliant mlkem768-x25519 key via mihomo age keygen-pq.
  • You can export the age-public-key from an age-secret-key via mihomo age convert <secret_key>.
  • You can decrypt an encrypted file via mihomo age decrypt <secret_key> <source_file> <target_file>. When <source_file> is -, it will read from standard input; when <target_file> is -, it will write to standard output.
  • You can encrypt an unencrypted file via mihomo age encrypt <public_key> <source_file> <target_file>. When <source_file> is -, it will read from standard input; when <target_file> is -, it will write to standard output.

Reference Implementations:

  • Golang: FiloSottile/age
  • Rust: str4d/rage
  • Typescript: FiloSottile/typage

docs.title: override

docs.title: override.additional-suffix
docs.text: Adds a fixed suffix to the node names.

docs.title: override.proxy-name
docs.text: Replaces content in node names, supporting regular expressions. pattern represents the content to be replaced, and target represents the replacement target.

docs.title: override.override-expr
docs.text:
This is a yq v4 style subset for expression-based configuration overrides. The array items are executed sequentially and take effect after the fixed-field overrides mentioned above (such as udp: true, etc.).

Supported path forms include ., .name, ."a.b", .["a.b"], .items[0], and .items[-1]; [] can traverse arrays or mappings. Assignment creates missing mappings and non-negative array indexes, but does not update through scalars.

Supported operations include =, |=, +=, -=, *=, del(.field), pipeline |, comma union, select, the // default operator, common comparisons, and boolean operations. Values support null / ~, booleans, decimal/hex integers, floats, double-quoted strings, arrays, and mappings with double-quoted keys.

Supported functions include length, keys, has, contains, select, reverse, sort, unique, flatten, any, all, map, map_values, filter, to_entries, from_entries, with_entries, any_c, all_c, test, sub, split, join, upcase, downcase, trim, tostring, tonumber, type, and not.

Limitations & Notes

  • Each item must ultimately produce exactly one mapping. Uncollected multiple results and del(.) are not supported.
  • Parentheses are mandatory when using pipelines (|) or and/or on the right side of an assignment.
  • from_entries only accepts an entries array with string keys.
  • sort only supports arrays of scalars. any/all only support their parameterless forms.
  • ==/!= compare the scalar textual value as per yq rules, rather than performing structural comparisons on arrays or mappings.
  • <, <=, >, >= only support numbers, strings, and null. Only false and null are treated as falsy values.
  • Standard function arguments must not yield multiple results; however, functions that receive expressions (such as map/filter) allow their arguments to yield result streams.
  • Function arguments can be separated by commas or semicolons.
  • Evaluation follows value semantics. Out-of-bounds reads do not extend the source array, and flatten/map_values on the right side do not implicitly modify the source path.
  • Use |= when you need to modify the source path. If there is no result on the right side, the target retains its original value instead of automatically creating null.
  • Mapping streams, keys, and entries transformations are sorted by key, as map[string]any does not preserve the original YAML key order.
  • Unsupported language features: Variables (as/$x), reduce, dynamic paths (.[$key]), recursive descent (..).
  • Unsupported external features: Multi-documents, file/environment access, YAML tag/style/comment handling, yq CLI arguments.
  • Any yq syntax, operators, or functions not explicitly listed above are also unsupported. Conditional branching can be achieved using select followed by multiple chained assignments.

docs.title: filter
docs.text: Filters nodes that match keywords or regular expressions. Use | to separate multiple regular expressions.

docs.title: exclude-filter
docs.text: Excludes nodes that match keywords or regular expressions. Use | to separate multiple regular expressions.

docs.title: exclude-type
docs.text:
Does not support regular expressions. Separated by |. Excludes nodes based on their proxy type.

The exclude-type of the provider uses the type field defined within the configuration file to perform the exclusion.

docs.title: payload
docs.text:
Content. Only takes effect when type is set to inline.

When http or file parsing fails, the payload can also be used as a fallback backup proxy list.

docs.title: name
docs.text: Required, such as google, must be unique.

docs.title: type
docs.text: Required, provider type, options are http / file / inline.

docs.title: url
docs.text: Must be configured if the type is http.

docs.title: path
docs.text:
Optional, file path, must be unique. If not provided, the MD5 of the URL will be used as the filename.

For security reasons, this path is restricted to only allow locations within HomeDir (configured with the -d startup parameter). If you want to store it in other locations, please specify additional safe paths by setting the SAFE_PATHS environment variable. The syntax of this environment variable is the same as the PATH environment variable parsing rules of this operating system (that is, it is separated by semicolons in Windows and by colons in other systems).

docs.title: interval
docs.text: The update interval for the provider, in seconds.

docs.title: proxy
docs.text: Download/update through the specified proxy.

docs.title: behavior
docs.text: Behavior, options are domain / ipcidr / classical, corresponding to different formats of rule-provider files. Please fill in according to the actual format.

docs.title: format
docs.text:
Format, options are yaml / text / mrs, default is yaml.

Currently, mrs behavior only supports domain / ipcidr. You can convert using mihomo convert-ruleset domain/ipcidr yaml/text XXX.yaml XXX.mrs.

docs.title: path-in-bundle
docs.text:
When this option is set, if the local file does not exist, it will be extracted from BundleMRS.7z in the Home Dir first. The value of this option specifies the path in BundleMRS.7z.

BundleMRS.7z can be downloaded from https://github.com/MetaCubeX/meta-rules-dat

docs.title: size-limit
docs.text: The maximum size of downloadable files is restricted, with the default being 0, which means no size limit; the unit is bytes (b)

docs.title: header
docs.text: Custom HTTP request headers.

docs.title: payload
docs.text: Content, only effective when type is inline

docs.title: classical
docs.text:
The classical type supports all types of routing rules (except for rule-set/sub-rule).

yamltext 
payload:
- DOMAIN-SUFFIX,google.com
- DOMAIN-KEYWORD,google
- DOMAIN,ad.com
- SRC-IP-CIDR,192.168.1.201/32
- IP-CIDR,127.0.0.0/8
- GEOIP,CN
- DST-PORT,80
- SRC-PORT,7777

DOMAIN-SUFFIX,google.com
DOMAIN-KEYWORD,google
DOMAIN,ad.com
SRC-IP-CIDR,192.168.1.201/32
IP-CIDR,127.0.0.0/8
GEOIP,CN
DST-PORT,80
SRC-PORT,7777

docs.title: domain
docs.text:
The content of the domain rule set must adhere to the clash wildcard.

yamltext 
payload:
- '.blogger.com'
- '*.*.microsoft.com'
- 'books.itunes.apple.com'

.blogger.com
*.*.microsoft.com
books.itunes.apple.com

docs.title: Routing Rules
docs.text:
rules:
- DOMAIN,ad.com,REJECT
- DOMAIN-SUFFIX,google.com,auto
- DOMAIN-KEYWORD,google,auto
- DOMAIN-WILDCARD,*.google.com,auto
- DOMAIN-REGEX,^abc.*com,PROXY
- GEOSITE,youtube,PROXY

- IP-CIDR,127.0.0.0/8,DIRECT,no-resolve
- IP-CIDR6,2620:0:2d0:200::7/32,auto
- IP-SUFFIX,8.8.8.8/24,PROXY
- IP-ASN,13335,DIRECT
- GEOIP,CN,DIRECT

- SRC-GEOIP,cn,DIRECT
- SRC-IP-ASN,9808,DIRECT
- SRC-IP-CIDR,192.168.1.201/32,DIRECT
- SRC-IP-SUFFIX,192.168.1.201/8,DIRECT

- DST-PORT,80,DIRECT
- SRC-PORT,7777,DIRECT

- IN-PORT,7890,PROXY
- IN-TYPE,SOCKS/HTTP,PROXY
- IN-USER,mihomo,PROXY
- IN-NAME,ss,PROXY
- REMATCH-NAME,rematch1,PROXY

- PROCESS-PATH,/usr/bin/wget,PROXY
- PROCESS-PATH,C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe,PROXY
- PROCESS-PATH-WILDCARD,/usr/*/wget,PROXY

- PROCESS-NAME-REGEX,curl$,PROXY
- PROCESS-NAME-REGEX,(?i)Telegram,PROXY
- PROCESS-NAME-REGEX,.*telegram.*,PROXY
- UID,1001,DIRECT

- NETWORK,udp,DIRECT
- DSCP,4,DIRECT

- RULE-SET,providername,proxy
- AND,((DOMAIN,baidu.com),(NETWORK,UDP)),DIRECT
- OR,((NETWORK,UDP),(DOMAIN,baidu.com)),REJECT
- NOT,((DOMAIN,baidu.com)),PROXY
- SUB-RULE,(NETWORK,tcp),sub-rule

- MATCH,auto

docs.title: Priority
docs.text:
Rules will be matched in order from top to bottom, with the rules at the top having higher priority than those below.

If the request is for UDP and the proxy node does not support UDP (for example, if the ss node does not have udp: true specified), it will continue to match downwards.

docs.title: DOMAIN
docs.text: Matches the full domain name.

docs.title: DOMAIN-SUFFIX
docs.text:
Matches the domain suffix.

For example, google.com matches www.google.com, mail.google.com, and google.com, but does not match content-google.com.

docs.title: DOMAIN-KEYWORD
docs.text: Matches using domain keywords.

docs.title: DOMAIN-WILDCARD
docs.text:
Wildcard matching, only supports * and ? wildcards.

Here * matches zero or more characters, ? matches exactly one character.

Note

Note that the wildcards here are different from the Clash format wildcards elsewhere in the configuration file.

docs.title: DOMAIN-REGEX
docs.text: Matches using regular expressions for domain names.

docs.title: GEOSITE
docs.text: Matches domains within a Geosite; some content is referenced from v2fly/domain-list-community.

docs.title: IP-CIDR & IP-CIDR6
docs.text: Matches IP address ranges; IP-CIDR and IP-CIDR6 have the same effect, with IP-CIDR6 being an alias.

docs.title: IP-SUFFIX
docs.text: Matches IP suffix ranges.

docs.title: IP-ASN
docs.text: Matches the ASN of the IP.

docs.title: GEOIP
docs.text: Matches the country code of the IP.

docs.title: SRC-GEOIP
docs.text: Matches the country code of the source IP.

docs.title: SRC-IP-ASN
docs.text: Matches the ASN of the source IP.

docs.title: SRC-IP-CIDR
docs.text: Matches the source IP address range.

docs.title: SRC-IP-SUFFIX
docs.text: Matches the source IP suffix range.

docs.title: DST-PORT
docs.text: Matches the target port range of the request.

docs.title: SRC-PORT
docs.text: Matches the source port range of the request.

docs.title: IN-PORT
docs.text: Matches the inbound port, allowing for port ranges.

docs.title: IN-TYPE
docs.text: Matches the inbound type.

docs.title: IN-USER
docs.text: Matches the inbound username, supporting multiple usernames separated by /.

docs.title: IN-NAME
docs.text: Matches the inbound name.

docs.title: REMATCH-NAME
docs.text: Matches the name written by a rematch outbound proxy.

docs.title: PROCESS-PATH
docs.text: Matches using the full process path.

docs.title: PROCESS-PATH-WILDCARD
docs.text:
Process path wildcard matching is used, supporting only * and ? wildcards.

Here * matches zero or more characters, ? matches exactly one character.

Note

Note that the wildcards here are different from the Clash format wildcards elsewhere in the configuration file.

docs.title: PROCESS-PATH-REGEX
docs.text: Matches using regular expressions for the process path.

docs.title: PROCESS-NAME
docs.text: Matches using the process name; on the Android platform, it can match package names.

docs.title: PROCESS-NAME-WILDCARD
docs.text:
Uses process name wildcard matching, supporting only * and ? wildcards. On the Android platform, it can also match package names.

Here * matches zero or more characters, ? matches exactly one character.

Note

Note that the wildcards here are different from the Clash format wildcards elsewhere in the configuration file.

docs.title: PROCESS-NAME-REGEX
docs.text: Matches using regular expressions for the process name; on the Android platform, it can match package names.

docs.title: UID
docs.text: Matches the Linux USER ID.

docs.title: NETWORK
docs.text: Matches tcp or udp.

docs.title: DSCP
docs.text: Matches the DSCP tag (only for tproxy UDP inbound).

docs.title: RULE-SET
docs.text: References a set of rules; configuration for rule-providers is required.

docs.title: AND & OR & NOT
docs.text:
LOGIC_TYPE,((payload1),(payload2)),Proxy

payload1 and payload2 are rule types and other payloads, such as DOMAIN,google.com.

Logical rules require careful use of parentheses.

docs.title: SUB-RULE
docs.text: Matches to sub-rules; careful use of parentheses is required.

docs.title: MATCH
docs.text: Matches all requests without conditions.

docs.title: no-resolve
docs.text:
Only supports rules regarding the target IP.

When domain matching begins for target IP rules, mihomo will trigger DNS resolution to check if the domain's target IP matches the rules. The no-resolve option can be selected to skip DNS resolution.

If DNS resolution was triggered in an earlier match, it will still match rules regarding target IP that have the no-resolve option added.

docs.title: src
docs.text:
Only supports rules regarding the target IP.

Converts target IP matching to source IP matching.

docs.title: Domain Sniffing
docs.text:
sniffer:
  enable: false
  force-dns-mapping: true
  parse-pure-ip: true
  override-destination: false
  sniff:
    HTTP:
      ports: [80, 8080-8880]
      override-destination: true
    TLS:
      ports: [443, 8443]
    QUIC:
      ports: [443, 8443]
  force-domain:
    - +.v2ex.com
  skip-domain:
    - Mijia Cloud
  skip-src-address:
    - 192.168.0.3/32
  skip-dst-address:
    - 192.168.0.3/32

docs.title: enable
docs.text: Whether to enable the sniffer.

docs.title: force-dns-mapping
docs.text: Force sniffing for traffic identified through redir-host mappings.

docs.title: parse-pure-ip
docs.text: Force sniffing for all traffic where no domain name was obtained.

docs.title: override-destination
docs.text: Whether to use the sniffing result as the actual destination. Defaults to true.

docs.title: sniff
docs.text:
Protocols to sniff. Only HTTP, TLS, and QUIC are supported.

  • ports: Port range
  • override-destination: Overrides the global override-destination setting

docs.title: force-domain
docs.text: List of domains that must be sniffed. Uses domain wildcards.

docs.title: skip-domain
docs.text: List of domains excluded from sniffing. Uses domain wildcards.

docs.title: skip-src-address
docs.text: List of source IP ranges excluded from sniffing.

docs.title: skip-dst-address
docs.text: List of destination IP ranges excluded from sniffing.

docs.title: Alpha Branch
docs.text:
Development version with support for new features.

Link

docs.title: Meta Branch
docs.text:
Stable version, updated monthly. Some documentation content may not be released promptly in the meta branch.

Link

    interval: 86400

    override:

  provider2:
    url: ""
    type: http
    interval: 86400
    health-check: {enable: true,url: "https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204",interval: 300}

      additional-prefix: "[provider2]"

proxies:
  - name: "Direct"
    type: direct
    udp: true

mixed-port: 7890
ipv6: true
allow-lan: true
unified-delay: false
tcp-concurrent: true
external-controller: 127.0.0.1:9090

    type: select

    type: select

docs.title: DNS
docs.text: Mihomo sends hijacked DNS traffic to its DNS module, allowing traffic from TUN, Redirect, or TProxy inbounds to map IP addresses back to their corresponding domain names.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
Mihomo uses yaml as the configuration file format.

yaml is case-sensitive and uses indentation to indicate hierarchical relationships. Tabs are not allowed for indentation; only spaces are permitted. The number of spaces used for indentation is not important, as long as elements at the same level are left-aligned.

docs.title: Comments
docs.text:
In yaml formatted files, comments start with "#" and extend to the end of the line. The "#" must be at the beginning of the line or preceded by a space; otherwise, it is not considered a comment.

port: 7890 # HTTP proxy port
socks-port: 7891
# SOCKS proxy port

docs.title: Objects
docs.text:
Key-value pairs in objects are represented using the colon structure key: value, with a space required after the colon. Indentation indicates hierarchical relationships.

Since the yaml format is a superset of json, you can directly use json syntax.

docs.title: Multi-line
docs.text:
tun:
  enable: true
  stack: system
  auto-route: true
  auto-detect-interface: true

docs.title: Multi-line JSON
docs.text:
tun: { 
  enable: true,
  stack: system,
  auto-route: true,
  auto-detect-interface: true
}

docs.title: Single-line JSON
docs.text: tun: { enable: true, stack: system, auto-route: true, auto-detect-interface: true }

docs.title: Full JSON
docs.text:
{
  "tun": {
    "enable": true,
    "stack": "system",
    "auto-route": true,
    "auto-detect-interface": true
  }
}

docs.title: Arrays
docs.text: Lines starting with - indicate the formation of an array, used for multiple values within an object.

docs.title: Multi-line Array
docs.text:
a:
  - b
  - c
  - d

docs.title: Single-line JSON Array
docs.text: a: [b, c, d]

docs.title: References
docs.text:
& is used for anchors and * for aliases, allowing for references. & establishes an anchor, while << indicates merging into the current data, and * is used to reference an anchor.

In the following example, since the key p does not exist in mihomo, it will be ignored during configuration parsing.

If there are duplicate items during merging, they will not be merged.

p: &p
  type: http
  interval: 3600
  health-check:
    enable: true
    url: https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204
    interval: 300

proxy-providers:
  provider1:
    <<: *p
    url: ""
    path: ./proxy_providers/provider1.yaml

  provider2:
    <<: *p
    type: file
    path: ./proxy_providers/provider2.yaml

This is equivalent to:

proxy-providers:
  provider1:
    type: http
    interval: 3600
    health-check:
      enable: true
      url: https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204
      interval: 300
    url: ""
    path: ./proxy_providers/provider1.yaml

  provider2:
    interval: 3600
    health-check:
      enable: true
      url: https://www.gstatic.com/generate_204
      interval: 300
    type: file
    path: ./proxy_providers/provider2.yaml

docs.title: Domain Wildcards
docs.text:
Note

The domain wildcard in this section is not the same as DOMAIN-WILDCARD in the routing rules.

docs.title: Wildcard *
docs.text:
Clash's wildcard * can only match one level of domain at a time.

*.baidu.com matches tieba.baidu.com but does not match 123.tieba.baidu.com or baidu.com.

* only matches hostnames without a . such as localhost.

docs.title: Wildcard +
docs.text:
The wildcard + is similar to DOMAIN-SUFFIX and can match multiple levels at once.

+.baidu.com matches tieba.baidu.com, 123.tieba.baidu.com, and baidu.com.

The wildcard + can only be used for prefix matching of domain names.

docs.title: Wildcard .
docs.text:
The wildcard . can match multiple levels at once.

.baidu.com matches tieba.baidu.com and 123.tieba.baidu.com, but does not match baidu.com.

The wildcard . can only be used for prefix matching of domain names.

docs.title: Usage Example
docs.text:
When using wildcards, you should wrap the content in quotes ' ' or " ".

fake-ip-filter:
- ".lan"
- "xbox.*.microsoft.com"
- "+.xboxlive.com"
- localhost.ptlogin2.qq.com

docs.title: Introducing Domain Sets
docs.text:
Warning

The rule-set only supports behavior as domain/classical.

fake-ip-filter:
- "rule-set:xxx"
- "geosite:xxx"

docs.title: Port Ranges
docs.text: Mihomo can use - to match port ranges, and / or , to separate multiple ports/port ranges.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Match ports 114 to 514 and 810 to 1919, as well as port 65530.

114-514/810-1919,65530

docs.title: Alpha and Meta Branch Differences
docs.text: The Alpha branch is the latest commit branch, while the Meta branch merges the code from the Alpha branch at regular intervals. The Meta branch is not necessarily more stable than the Alpha branch.

docs.title: Which file should I download?
docs.text:
Inrelease, the filename of each package includes several pieces of information, including:

  • Program name (mihomo)
  • Operating system (e.g., android, darwin, freebsd, linux, windows, etc.)
  • Architecture (e.g., 386, amd64, arm32v7, arm64, etc.)
  • Compilation method:
    • v1/2/3: only for AMD64 platforms, used to mark CPU instruction set level
    • default(not specified in file name): Default version compiled with GOAMD64=v3 tag.
    • compatible: Compiled with GOAMD64=v1 tag for compatibility with specific OS or architecture.
    • go120: Compiled with Golang1.20 for compatibility with specific OS or architecture. Without the go tag, the code is compiled using the latest Golang version.
    • abi1/2: ABI version for loongarch64
  • Compile branch (e.g., alpha)
  • Git hash value of the commit (e.g., f90066f)

You can choose the executable file you need based on these pieces of information.

Check details between different architectural levels here.

Check details of system compatibility information about Golang version 1.20 here.

Learn more about loongarch64 ABI versions here.

For macOS users: According to the Go wiki, Go 1.25 no longer supports macOS 11. macOS 11 users are advised to download the binary with the go124 tag, macOS 10.15 users are advised to download the binary with the go122 tag, and macOS 10.13 users are advised to download the binary with the go120 tag.

For macOS users: Currently, all official builds support macOS 10.13 and later (Unofficial builds are not guaranteed to support this; we maintain our own forked version of Golang for compiling.)

For Windows users: Currently, all official builds support Windows 7 and higher. (Unofficial builds are not guaranteed to support this; we maintain our own forked version of Golang for compiling.)

For Linux users: According to the go wiki, Go 1.24 only supports kernels 3.2 and higher. For kernels 2.6.32 to 3.1, please download the binary tagged go123.
